Abase.text: Being brought to your base (low point) can be humiliating.

Abate.text: If you don't use bait you reduce your odds in fishing.

Abdicate.text: "ab-" (away) + "dic-" (speak) + "-ate" (verb) = to formally speak away your power.

Aberration.text: "ab-" (away) + "err" (wander) = wandering away from what is normal.

Abet.text: A bet placed on someone encourages them to succeed.

Abhor.text: A horror - you hate it and shrink away from it.

Abide.text: You bide your time, enduring or putting up with the situation.

Abject.text: "ab-" (away) + "ject" (throw) = thrown away into a wretched state.

Abjure.text: "ab-" (away) + "jur" (swear) = to swear away or legally renounce.

Abnegation.text: "ab-" (away) + "neg" (deny) = the act of denying yourself something.

Abridge.text: A bridge connects two points, making the journey shorter.

Abrogate.text: A broken gate means the security rules have been abolished or repealed.

Abscond.text: Abscond with the scones - running away secretly with the stolen goods.

Absolution.text: The absolute solution to guilt is being forgiven completely.

Abstain.text: You abstain from wine so it doesn't stain your shirt.

Abstruse.text: Sounds like obstructs - the complex meaning obstructs your understanding.

Accolade.text: From Latin "ad" (to) + "collum" (neck). Originally a ceremonial embrace around the neck when conferring knighthood.

Accost.text: From Latin "ad costam" (to the rib/side). To aggressively come right up to someone's side and confront them.

Accretion.text: A creation built up gradually layer by layer.

Acerbic.text: An acerbic comment is acidic and bites.

Acquiesce.text: Acquire quiet - you stay quiet and just agree to it.

Acrimony.text: A crime on me - leaves you with bitterness and ill will.

Acumen.text: IQ men - men with high IQs have great mental sharpness.

Acute.text: A cute angle is sharp and precise.

Adamant.text: From Greek adamas, meaning "uncuttable" (the same root as diamond). Unyielding and unbreakable.

Adept.text: A depth of knowledge makes you highly skilled.

Adroit.text: From French à droit meaning "to the right." The right hand was historically considered the skilled and dexterous one.

Adulation.text: Adult infatuation - excessive flattery and worship.

Adumbrate.text: "ad-" (toward) + "umbr" (shadow) = to cast a shadow over, or outline vaguely.

Advocate.text: "ad-" (toward) + "voc" (voice) = to add your voice toward a cause.

Affable.text: A fable - the grandpa telling a fable is friendly and easy to talk to.

Affinity.text: Infinity - an infinite, natural connection or liking for something.

Affront.text: In front - an insult right in front of your face.

Aggregate.text: "ag-" (toward) + "greg" (flock) = to bring the flock together into a whole.

Aggrieved.text: You feel aggrieved when someone causes you grief.

Agnostic.text: "a-" (without) + "gnos" (knowledge) = claiming to be without knowledge (usually of God).

Alacrity.text: Sounds like electricity - moving with electric speed and readiness.

Allay.text: To lay a baby down soothes and calms it.

Alleviate.text: "ad-" (to) + "lev" (light) = to make something lighter or less severe.

Aloof.text: A roof - sitting alone on a roof, emotionally detached from everyone below.

Altercation.text: When things alter (change) for the worse, a noisy argument starts.

Amalgamate.text: A mall - a mall brings many different stores together into one place.

Ambivalent.text: "ambi-" (both) + "val" (strength/feeling) = having strong feelings in both directions.

Ameliorate.text: A million rate - taking a bad rating and improving it to a million.

Amenable.text: Amen-able - someone who frequently says "amen" (yes) and is easily persuaded.

Anachronistic.text: "ana-" (backward/against) + "chron" (time) = placed in the wrong time period.

Anarchist.text: "an-" (without) + "arch" (ruler) = one who believes in no government.

Anathema.text: A nasty asthma - something you vehemently despise and avoid.

Annul.text: A null - making a contract legally null and void.

Anomaly.text: "an-" (not) + "homos" (same) = not the same as the normal rule.

Antediluvian.text: "ante-" (before) + "diluvium" (flood) = literally "before the biblical flood," meaning ancient.

Anthology.text: Greek anthos (flower) + logia (collection): a bouquet metaphor for chosen writings.

Apocryphal.text: A pocket full of lies - a story of doubtful authenticity.

Appease.text: To bring someone to a state of peace.

Appraise.text: You offer praise when you determine something has high value.

Apprehend.text: Up your hand - what cops say when they arrest you.

Approbation.text: Approve a nation - official approval or praise.

Appropriate.text: A property - to take something for your own use.

Arbiter.text: An arbiter will bite down on the arguments and make the final decision.

Arboreal.text: From Latin arbor (tree). Relating to trees.

Arcane.text: Ark cane - Indiana Jones needed a cane to find the lost Ark because its location was highly mysterious and known only to a few.

Ardor.text: Harder - working harder because you have intense passion.

Arid.text: A rid - you rid the desert of all its water.

Arrogate.text: Arrow gate - taking over the castle gate with arrows to claim it without justification.

Ascertain.text: As certain - to make yourself absolutely certain of the facts.

Ascetic.text: Acidic - a monk drinking acidic water instead of wine to practice severe self-discipline.

Ascribe.text: "ad-" (to) + "scribere" (write) = to write someone's name to a quote, crediting them.

Aspersion.text: Ass portion - talking about the worst portion of someone to damage their reputation.

Assail.text: A sail - pirate ships use a sail to suddenly attack you.

Assiduous.text: Ass in dust - working so hard and persistently that your donkey is leaving a dust trail.

Assuage.text: A sausage - feeding a hungry, angry dog a sausage to calm and satisfy it.

Astute.text: A student - a good student is sharp and clever.

Atone.text: At one - making up for your sins so you are back "at one" with God.

Atrophy.text: A trophy - if you just stare at a trophy and stop training, your muscles will waste away.

Audacious.text: All day shush - if you shush the teacher all day, you are boldly disrespectful.

Augment.text: Argument - you augment (add to) your essay to win the argument.

Auspicious.text: Awesome space - entering an awesome space is a sign of future success.

Austere.text: Out steer - steering out of the fun lane into a strict, severe, undecorated lifestyle.

Avarice.text: Have a rice - the greedy rich man wants to have every single grain of rice for himself.

Balk.text: A dog barks when it balks (refuses to move forward).

Banal.text: Ban all - we should ban all boring, unoriginal movies.

Bane.text: Batman's enemy Bane is the cause of his great distress.

Bashful.text: If you are shy, you might nervously bash your toe into the ground.

Beguile.text: "be-" (completely) + "guile" (trickery) = to charm someone in a deceptive way.

Bequeath.text: Beneath - leaving your possessions to the generation beneath you.

Berate.text: To give someone a B rate (a bad grade) and scold them angrily for it.

Bereft.text: He left - feeling utterly deprived and sad because he left.

Beseech.text: Bee speech - begging the bees not to sting you.

Bilk.text: Milk - the scammer will milk you for all your money.

Blandish.text: Bland dish - flattering the chef by saying the bland dish is actually delicious.

Blight.text: B-light - blocking out the light causes the crops to die.

Boisterous.text: Boys terrorize - loud, energetic boys terrorizing the playground.

Bombastic.text: Bomb - inflating your speech with explosive words that lack actual substance.

Boon.text: Boom! - an explosive stroke of good luck or benefit.

Brazen.text: Brass - acting as bold and hard as brass without any shame.

Brusque.text: Brush - brushing someone off with a quick, rude reply.

Buffet.text: A buff guy hitting you repeatedly.

Burnish.text: Burn - rubbing the metal so fast it burns and becomes highly polished.

Buttress.text: Butt rest - a support structure that acts as a rest for the building.

Cacophony.text: "kakos" (bad) + "phon" (sound) = a harsh, bad mixture of sounds.

Cajole.text: Cage hole - coaxing the scared puppy out of the cage hole with a treat.

Calamity.text: Calm am I? T(ee) - No, I am not calm, it's a disaster!

Callous.text: Having physical calluses on your hands makes them hard; having a callous personality means your heart is hard and unfeeling.

Calumny.text: Column - writing a gossip column full of slander to ruin someone's reputation.

Candor.text: Can do - being completely open and honest about what you can do.

Canny.text: Can he? - Yes, he can, because he's shrewd and clever.

Canvas.text: Canvas fabric was originally made from cannabis (hemp).

Capacious.text: Capacity - having a massive capacity; very roomy.

Capitulate.text: "caput" (head) = to give up your head to the enemy, to surrender.

Capricious.text: Capri Sun - a toddler's mood is capricious; one second they are happy, the next they are crying because they dropped their Capri Sun.

Carouse.text: Car house - driving your car into the house because you were partying and drinking noisily.

Carp.text: A carp fish just swims around complaining and nitpicking.

Caucus.text: Cactus - politicians gathering around a spiky cactus to hold a closed political meeting.

Caustic.text: Cause sick - drinking a harsh chemical will cause you to be sick.

Cavort.text: Cave fort - jumping and dancing around excitedly in your childhood cave fort.

Censure.text: Censor - the government officially criticizes and censors the journalist.

Chastise.text: Chase ties - the boss will chase you down and scold you for not wearing a tie.

Chide.text: Child - how you gently scold a child for making a mistake.

Circuitous.text: "circum" (around) + "ire" (go) = going in a circular, indirect route.

Circumlocution.text: "circum" (around) + "loc" (speak) = talking around the subject to avoid the point.

Circumscribed.text: "circum" (around) + "scribere" (write/draw) = drawing a line around something to restrict it.

Circumspect.text: "circum" (around) + "spect" (look) = looking around carefully before acting; cautious.

Circumvent.text: "circum" (around) + "vent" (go) = to find a way around an obstacle.

Clairvoyant.text: "clair" (clear) + "voyant" (seeing) = having clear vision of the future.

Clamor.text: Claim more - the angry mob is loudly shouting to claim more rights.

Clandestine.text: Clan destiny - the secret clan held a hidden meeting to discuss their destiny.

Cleave.text: Meat cleaver - a heavy knife used to split things apart.

Clemency.text: Lemon tea - giving the sore-throated prisoner a warm lemon tea as an act of mercy.

Cloying.text: Clawing - romantic words that are so overly sweet you want to claw your ears off.

Coagulate.text: Colgate - toothpaste thickens and solidifies if left out.

Coalesce.text: Fuse into one: co- (together) + alescere (to grow)-parts grow together into a single mass.

Coerce.text: Force - using force or threats to get someone to do something.

Cogent.text: Co-agent - your FBI co-agent presents a clear, logical, and convincing argument.

Colloquial.text: "col-" (together) + "loqu" (speak) = how people speak together in everyday conversation.

Collusion.text: "col-" (together) + "ludere" (play/deceive) = playing together in a secret, deceitful plot.

Commendation.text: Recommend a nation - giving official praise.

Complacency.text: Come play, senor - relaxing and playing instead of staying alert to danger.

Complement.text: "com-" (together) + "plere" (fill) = to fill up together, completing the whole.

Complicit.text: "com-" (together) + "plic" (fold) = folded together into an illegal act.

Compliment.text: Latin complementum (what completes); courtesy praise - remember: I like compliments.

Conciliatory.text: Council - bringing the arguing sides to the council to make peace.

Concise.text: "con-" (completely) + "caedere" (cut) = cut down to just the essential words.

Concoct.text: Cocktail - mixing ingredients together to invent a new cocktail.

Concomitant.text: Comet tent - if a comet hits your tent, disaster is a naturally accompanying result.

Condone.text: Con done - the convict is done with his sentence, so you overlook his past offense.

Conduit.text: Can do it - the pipe is the channel that can do the job of moving the water.

Confidant.text: "con-" (with) + "fid" (trust) = the person you completely trust with your secrets.

Conflagration.text: Flag ration - a massive fire burned through the military base, so they had to ration their remaining flags.
